input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **Command Line Tools**: `xcode-select --install`
- On first launch, accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Discover the required version: check the [.NET MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) — it lists the minimum Xcode version per .NET release.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download)
- Download the latest **.NET SDK** (currently .NET 9). Choose the **macOS installer** (Arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in the iOS, Android, and platform-specific workloads.
- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Verify installed workloads: `dotnet workload list`

## 4. Install Android SDK Components

The MAUI workload bundles the Android SDK, but you need to ensure the right pieces are in place:

- **JDK**: The MAUI workload installs Microsoft's OpenJDK build automatically. Verify with `dotnet android sdk info`.
- **Android SDK/NDK**: Auto-provisioned on first build. To manage manually, install [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) and use its SDK Manager.
- Discover required Android API levels: `dotnet workload install maui` output will show what it pulls, or check [Microsoft's MAUI docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation).
- Accept Android licenses: `dotnet android sdk accept-licenses` (or via Android Studio).

## 5. Install an IDE

Pick one (or both):

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/) + Extensions marketplace |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (paid, full MAUI support) |

> **Note:** Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with the **.NET MAUI Extension** is now Microsoft's recommended path on macOS.

## 6. Verify Everything

```bash
# 1. SDK & workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui" (and sub-workloads)

# 2. Create & build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# 3. iOS (requires Xcode + simulator)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
# Launch in simulator:
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios

# 4. Android (requires emulator or device)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
# Launch in emulator:
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Simulators & Emulators

- **iOS Simulator**: Ships with Xcode. Manage via `xcrun simctl list devices` or Xcode → Settings → Platforms.
- **Android Emulator**: Create via Android Studio → Device Manager, or CLI: `avdmanager create avd`.

---

**Key official references:**
- [.NET MAUI Getting Started](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)
- [.NET MAUI Support Policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) (version compatibility matrix)
- [.NET MAUI with VS Code](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation?tabs=visual-studio-code)
